---
title: "GitHub, Posit Connect & CodeSpaces"
subtitle: "Static Portfolios, Environment Caching, and Cloud Workspaces"
author: "Brian Yandell ([_byandell.github.io_](https://byandell.github.io))"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "GitHub, Posit Connect & CodeSpaces"
    highlight-style: github
---

# GitHub Pages & Portfolios {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## GitHub Pages & Blogging {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Static Portfolio Hosting
- **[GitHub Pages](https://docs.github.com/en/pages)**: Free static web hosting directly from GitHub repositories.
- **Main Portfolio**: [byandell.github.io](https://byandell.github.io) ([source](https://github.com/byandell/byandell.github.io)), modeled on [Jekyll Now](https://github.com/barryclark/jekyll-now).
- **Subpages** (separate repos):
  - [Documentation](https://byandell.github.io/Documentation) ([source](https://github.com/byandell/Documentation)): Digital Tools
  - [geyser](https://byandell.github.io/geyser/) ([source](https://github.com/byandell/geyser)): Shiny App with Modules
  - [landmapyr](https://byandell-envsys.github.io/landmapyr/) ([source](https://github.com/byandell-envsys/landmapyr)): Land Mapping Package
  - [esiil-stars](https://byandell.github.io/esiil-stars) ([source](https://github.com/byandell/esiil-stars)): ESIIL Stars Training
  - [ESIIL](https://byandell.github.io/ESIIL) ([source](https://github.com/byandell/ESIIL)): ESIIL Research
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-note}
### Core References
- [GitHub Pages Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/pages)
- [Publishing Project Docs](https://github.blog/developer-skills/github/publish-your-project-documentation-with-github-pages/)
- [Jekyll Now Blog Setup](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2014/08/build-blog-jekyll-github-pages/)
- [GitHub Page Themes](https://pages.github.com/themes/) & [Other Themes](https://github.com/barryclark/jekyll-now/#other-forkable-themes)
- [Portfolio Creation Guide](https://cu-esiil-edu.github.io/esiil-learning-portal/shortcourse/pages/03-git-github/03-github-portfolio/01-create-portfolio-website.html) (ESIIL)
- [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions)
:::
:::
:::

## Jekyll, MkDocs & Git Resources {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Static Generators & Themes
- **Jekyll**: Default Ruby-based builder used by GitHub Pages. Fast setup using forkable themes.
- **[MkDocs](https://github.com/mkdocs/mkdocs/tree/master)**: Advanced Python-based alternative for technical docs.
  - Deploys layouts using themes like [Material for MkDocs](https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material/publishing-your-site/).
  - See [Deploying your docs](https://www.mkdocs.org/user-guide/deploying-your-docs/).
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Git Troubleshooting & Help
- **[Happy Git with R](https://happygitwithr.com/)**: Standard resource for onboarding and troubleshooting.
  - [Get Started with GitHub](https://happygitwithr.com/usage-intro#usage-intro)
  - [Git Troubleshooting](https://happygitwithr.com/troubleshooting)
- **UW-Madison DataScience**:
  - [GitHub Links](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/R_for_data_sciences/blob/master/organize/github.md)
- **Git Clients & Setup**:
  - [Install Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/install/windows) (Git-SCM)
  - [Install Git Guides](https://github.com/git-guides/install-git)
:::
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
### CI/CD Workflow Security
OAuth authentication errors can prevent git pushes if you modify GitHub Actions files (`.github/workflows/`). See the [TutorialPedia resolution guide](https://www.tutorialpedia.org/blog/how-to-resolve-refusing-to-allow-an-oauth-app-to-create-or-update-workflow-on-git-push/) to resolve permission issues.
:::

# Posit Connect Hosting {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Posit Connect & Package Locking {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Enterprise Sharing Platform
- **[Posit Connect](https://posit.co/products/enterprise/connect/)**: Deployment platform to host R Shiny applications, Quarto documents, and python web models.
- **Environment Replication**: Connect uses the **`renv`** package to capture local project dependencies and rebuild them on the server.
- **GitHub Integration**: Packages installed via standard devtools (`devtools::install_github`) can lead to package resolution issues on compilation.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-note}
### Documentation Links
- [renv Home Page](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/)
- [Manage dependencies with renv](https://www.r-bloggers.com/2021/03/new-on-techguides-manage-dependencies-with-renv/)
- [Using renv with Connect](https://pkgs.rstudio.com/renv/articles/rsconnect.html)
- [Posit Package Management](https://docs.posit.co/connect/admin/r/package-management/)
:::
:::
:::

## Resolving Deployment Errors {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### The Unknown Source Bug
- **Symptoms**: RStudio displays `Deployment error: unknown package source`.
- **Cause**: Packages installed via `devtools` may lack complete lockfile metadata. Connect is unable to determine the precise repository source, and deployment fails.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### The renv Caching Solution
Force `renv` to explicitly write the GitHub source link to the lockfile:
```R
# Install package through renv API
renv::install("github_user/package_name")

# Snapshot changes to renv.lock
renv::snapshot()
```

If it still fails, record the source metadata manually:
```R
renv::record("github_user/package_name")
```
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
Always inspect your `renv.lock` file before deploying to confirm all external GitHub package metadata has been saved cleanly.
:::

# GitHub Codespaces {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Cloud-Hosted Containers {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Dynamic Cloud Workspaces
- **[GitHub Codespaces](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/overview)**: Instant, preconfigured container development workspaces hosted in the cloud.
- **Fast Onboarding**: Integrates seamlessly with Git repositories, preloading extensions, scripts, and runtime dependencies.
- **VS Code Integration**: Develop directly in your web browser or connect via local Visual Studio Code remote editors.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-important}
### Resource Conservation
Always **stop** your codespaces when done! Inactive but running codespaces continue to consume subscription hours, memory quotas, and active storage allocations.
:::
:::
:::

### Useful Links:
- [GitHub Codespaces Overview](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/overview) & [Documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces)
- [Codespaces Quickstart](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/getting-started/quickstart)
- [Visual Studio Code Codespaces Guide](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/remote/codespaces)
- [Stopping and starting a codespace](https://docs.github.com/en/codespaces/developing-in-a-codespace/stopping-and-starting-a-codespace#stopping-a-codespace)
